what_it_measures: thyroid-stimulating hormone, made by the pituitary gland to regulate the thyroid
low_meaning: a low TSH can be associated with an overactive thyroid (hyperthyroidism)
high_meaning: a high TSH can be associated with an underactive thyroid (hypothyroidism)
dietary_note: adequate iodine intake is commonly discussed for thyroid health, though needs vary by individual

what_it_measures: the active, unbound form of triiodothyronine (T3), a thyroid hormone that regulates metabolism
low_meaning: a low free T3 can be associated with an underactive thyroid or with non-thyroid illness
high_meaning: a high free T3 can be associated with an overactive thyroid
dietary_note: thyroid-related nutrition is best individualised with a clinician

what_it_measures: the active, unbound form of thyroxine (T4), the main hormone the thyroid releases
low_meaning: a low free T4 can be associated with an underactive thyroid (hypothyroidism)
high_meaning: a high free T4 can be associated with an overactive thyroid (hyperthyroidism)
dietary_note: thyroid-related nutrition is best individualised with a clinician
